content.claim: A 1990s national survey on adoption of 6-year gimnázium tracks found differential institutional engagement: Budapest schools responded at 45.4% versus rural schools at 29.5%, indicating a long-standing urban/rural divergence in structural change adoption.
content.context: Fresh live research through the School network planning lens
limitations: Survey data is from 1990s, not current; institutional response rates do not directly measure current capacity or student access
Response asymmetry reflects institutional engagement with policy change, not necessarily current uptake or network distribution
No comparable recent survey data available to assess whether urban/rural divergence has persisted or narrowed
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
